blocks.text: These Payment Terms and Conditions ("Terms") constitute a legal agreement between You (the Merchant) and Razorpay PA ( as defined below ). You acknowledge that the payment aggregation business, assets, and all associated contractual obligations pertaining to the Services will stand legally transferred from Razorpay Software Private Limited ("Transferor Entity") to and vested in Razorpay Payments Private Limited ("Transferee Entity") pursuant to a corporate demerger and regulatory approvals, operationalized from January 1, 2026 (the "Transfer Date"). You agree that from the Transfer Date, the rights and obligations under these Terms shall stand transferred to the Transferee Entity. All references to "Razorpay PA", "us," or "we," or "our " under these Terms will automatically and exclusively mean and refer to the Transferor Entity till the Transfer Date and to the Transferee Entity from the Transfer Date. Your continued access to or use of the Services on or after the Transfer Date constitutes Your express and irrevocable consent to the novation of this Agreement to the Transferee Entity, which shall be Your sole contracting party from the Transfer Date. The Terms, constituted of Part A: General Terms and Conditions and Part B: Specific Terms and Conditions, govern Your access to and use of Razorpay PA's payment services, including payment aggregation, point of sale devices and soundboxes ("Devices"), technology, software, analytics or any other services, tools or products offered or made available by Razorpay PA and/or its Facility Providers ("Services"). The Services may be offered or made available to You via the Website, mobile applications, software, APIs, social media, or other access channels ("Platform"). Your use of the Platform and Services is also subject to the Website Terms of Use, which are incorporated herein by reference. "You", "Your(s)", "Yourself" or "Merchant" refers to a merchant, (whether a non-registered individual/sole proprietor or corporate body), who registers for use, or accesses the Platform or Services. The Services provided by Razorpay PA through the Platform are available and are appropriate only for use in India. If You are a Merchant based outside of India collecting monies from Indian buyers, the provision of these Services is governed by Part A (General Terms and Conditions) and the combined provisions of Part B, Part I (Payment Aggregation Services), and Part B, Part IB (Specific Terms for Cross-Border Outward Transactions), with Part IB superseding any conflicting general provisions.

blocks.text: Please read these Terms carefully before accessing the Platform or using the Services. By accessing, registering for or using the Platform or any of the Services, You agree to be bound by these Terms, including our Privacy Policy , Website Terms of Use and any other policy applicable to the Services received via the Platform. If You do not agree to these Terms or do not wish to be bound by these Terms, You must immediately stop/terminate the use of the Services and/or refrain from accessing the Platform. Razorpay PA reserves the right to amend or otherwise modify the Terms at any time by posting an updated version on the website. The updated Terms shall take effect immediately upon posting. In addition to posting the updated Terms on the Platform, Razorpay PA may notify You of material changes via email or a prominent notification within the Platform. However, failure to receive such a notification shall not affect the immediate enforceability of the updated Terms. It is Your responsibility to review these Terms periodically for updates/amendments. Your continued access of the Platform or use of the Services signifies Your assent/ratification of the updated or modified Terms. If You object to these Terms or any subsequent modifications to these Terms in any way, Your only recourse is to immediately terminate/stop the use of the Services.

blocks.text: We may require You to agree to additional terms in connection with specific Services, provided by Razorpay PA, that You may avail from time to time. You agree to be bound by supplemental terms of any specific Service that You access or use via our Platform and/or are available by hyperlink on our Platform. We may ask You to agree to those supplemental terms by way of ‘acceptance'. Should You choose to avail any specific Service, You may be required to complete forms and provide additional data/information. You hereby give your consent for us to store and use the data/information You provide on the Platform during (i) the initial sign up/registration process and (ii) registration or onboarding for any specific Service in future. You hereby further give Your consent for us to pre-fill forms for the registration or onboarding process of any specific Service with such data/information provided. You acknowledge and agree that we reserve the right to verify, and re-verify where applicable, the data/information You provide in relation to any specific Service. Your right to access and use any specific Service is subject to successful completion, at our sole discretion, of registration or onboarding process for that specific Service. To the extent these Terms are inconsistent with any supplemental terms for a specific Service, then those specific terms shall prevail over these Terms.

blocks.text: "Chargeback" shall mean the reversal (such reversal being requested by a Facility Provider pursuant to a request from the Facility Provider's customer) of the debit of the Transaction Amount that was charged by You, where the reversal is approved by the Facility Provider following examination of the Transaction related documents and information furnished by You, consequently resulting in Razorpay PA being charged the Transaction Amount and charges, penalties or fines associated with processing the Chargeback.

blocks.text: 1.1 We (and our successors and licensors, as applicable) remain the sole owner of all rights, title and interest in the Services, including the Platform, and all underlying intellectual property rights (whether registered or not). Razorpay PA grants You a personal, revocable, non-exclusive, non-transferable, limited right to access the Platform and make use of the Platform and the Services in accordance with the Terms. You shall not remove, obscure, or alter any proprietary rights notices (including trademark and copyright notices), which may be affixed to or contained within the Services. We reserve all rights not granted under the Terms. We (and our successors and licensors, as applicable) retain our rights in and to trademarks, trade names, service marks, logos, domain names, and other distinctive brand features ("marks") owned or used by us in the course of our business. You do not have the right to use any of our marks without explicit consent from us. You shall not download, copy, create a derivative work, modify, reverse engineer, reverse assemble, transmit or otherwise attempt to discover any source code, sell, assign, sub-license, grant a security interest in or otherwise transfer any right in the Services or marks. You further acknowledge and agree that the Services may contain information that is designated confidential by us and You shall not disclose such information without our prior written consent.

You shall not engage, integrate, or route any payments through a third-party payment orchestrator not explicitly approved by Razorpay PA. Any breach of this clause, including use of an unapproved third-party orchestrator, shall entitle Razorpay PA to immediately suspend and/or terminate the Services without any liability to Razorpay PA.

blocks.text: FEES

clause_path: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS
FEES
blocks.clause_path_text: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES

blocks.text: 3.1 Applicable fees for the provision of Services/Devices shall be levied by Razorpay PA from time to time. You agree that the fees shall be charged according to the manner, rates and frequency determined by Razorpay PA. Razorpay PA reserves the right to update the amount of the fees charged at its sole discretion. Razorpay PA fees allow access to the entire suite of payments products, dashboard and custom reports, and includes MDR charges, if any, for payment instruments as prescribed under Applicable Law. For clarity, Razorpay PA fees include zero MDR for Rupay Debit Cards and UPI transactions.

You agree that the fees shall be charged according to the manner, rates and frequency determined by Razorpay PA. Razorpay PA reserves the right to update the amount of the fees charged at its sole discretion. Razorpay PA fees allow access to the entire suite of payments products, dashboard and custom reports, and includes MDR charges, if any, for payment instruments as prescribed under Applicable Law. For clarity, Razorpay PA fees include zero MDR for Rupay Debit Cards and UPI transactions.

blocks.text: 3.2 Fees are exclusive of applicable taxes and Razorpay PA will charge such applicable taxes on the fees from time to time. You agree that any statutory variations in applicable taxes during the subsistence of these Terms shall be borne by You.
blocks.text_without_marker: Fees are exclusive of applicable taxes and Razorpay PA will charge such applicable taxes on the fees from time to time. You agree that any statutory variations in applicable taxes during the subsistence of these Terms shall be borne by You.

FEES

blocks.text: 3.3 We will raise monthly invoices in respect of fees charged for Services provided during such months. Invoices are available on the dashboard on a monthly basis. Any dispute in respect of an invoice must be communicated by You to us via a notice no later than ten (10) days from the date of the invoice, failing which such invoices would be deemed to be accepted by You. For disputed invoices, Razorpay PA shall use good faith efforts to reconcile any reasonably disputed amounts.

For disputed invoices, Razorpay PA shall use good faith efforts to reconcile any reasonably disputed amounts.

blocks.text: 3.4 You shall be responsible for daily reconciliation of all the transactions processed. In case of discrepancies, You shall report to Razorpay PA regarding such discrepancy within three (3) days upon the receipt of the fund settlements. Razorpay PA shall not be liable for any reconciliation issue if the same is highlighted by You to Razorpay PA after such time.

FEES
blocks.clause_path_text: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES > 3.4

blocks.text: 3.5 For fees deducted upfront before provision of the specific Service, it is agreed that if You deposit applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in respect of invoices received by You) and furnish to Razorpay PA Form 16-A in respect of such taxes paid, then Razorpay PA shall reimburse to You, on a quarterly basis, the amount in respect of such taxes paid. In all other cases, with respect to invoices received by You, at the time of payment of the Fees, You will withhold applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in case LTDC is provided as per the LTDC issued). You shall deposit the withheld taxes with the government treasury, file the statutorily mandated returns and furnish the requisite tax deduction certificate (Form 16-A) to Razorpay PA within one hundred and eighty (180) days so as to enable Razorpay PA to obtain full credit for the taxes deducted at source.

In all other cases, with respect to invoices received by You, at the time of payment of the Fees, You will withhold applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in case LTDC is provided as per the LTDC issued). blocks.text: 3.6 If You operate as an e-commerce operator, facilitating the sale of goods or provisions of services of a resident e-commerce participant, You shall evaluate and comply with the requirements of TDS under Section 19.40 of the Income Tax Act, 1961. You, as an e-commerce operator, will withhold tax under Section 19.40 of the Act and deposit the same within the applicable timelines, including carrying out all the necessary compliances as prescribed under the Income Tax Act, 1961. As the payment service provider, Razorpay PA would not be obligated to deduct tax under Section 19.40 and the same will be Your responsibility as the e-commerce operator. For the purposes of this clause 3.5A, "e-commerce operator" and "e-commerce participant" shall have the meaning assigned to them in section 19.40 of the Income Tax Act, 1961.

blocks.text: 3.7 You shall be solely responsible for updating Your GST registration number on the Razorpay PA dashboard before Razorpay PA generates the invoice and shall also submit the GST certificate as part of KYC. Razorpay PA will raise a GST tax invoice and report the transactions in the GST returns based on the information provided by You. The GST returns will be filed as per the statutory timelines, to enable You to avail appropriate input tax credit. Razorpay PA shall not be responsible for any mistake and or misrepresentation by You in updating the GST number and other particulars as per the GST certificate. Further, any liability raised on Razorpay PA by the GST authorities due to incorrect information provided by You or deliberate withholding of any statutory information by You shall be recovered by Razorpay PA from You.

blocks.text: 3.8 In order for Razorpay PA to issue a proper B2B tax invoice under the GST law and to ensure GST input credit is available to You, Razorpay PA shall record Your correct GSTIN. Towards this, You are advised to verify Your GSTIN and registered address captured within the account maintained with Razorpay PA at periodical intervals and correct the same wherever necessary. In the event, correct GSTIN is not updated in Your account maintained with Razorpay PA, then You shall be solely responsible in respect thereof and Razorpay PA shall not be liable to accommodate any request for revision of invoice and / or amendment to GST reporting.

blocks.text: 13.1 You agree to comply with all applicable anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws which prohibit officials, representatives, agents or any other person associated with or acting on behalf of You from giving, offering, promising to offer, receiving/ accepting or acting in any other manner so as to induce a payment, gift, hospitality or anything else of value (either directly or indirectly) whether from within the country or from abroad to government officials, public servants, regulatory bodies, judicial authorities, persons in positions of authority, elected or contesting electoral candidates, political parties or office bearers thereof or any other third party or person in order to obtain an improper commercial/ business advantage of any kind. Government officials include any government employee, candidate for public office, an employee of government- owned or government–controlled companies, public international organisations and political parties. blocks.text: e. You may choose to purchase Fee Credits from Razorpay PA in respect of the online payment aggregation Services. It is agreed that in respect of each Transaction, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct an amount equivalent to Razorpay PA fees along with applicable taxes from the Fee Credit. You agree that if sufficient funds are not available in the Fee Credits, then Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct fees along with applicable taxes from the Transaction Amount. "Fee Credits" are the credits using which You may receive the full settlement amount without any fee deduction. For example, if You have a Fee Credit of INR 100 then all the Transactions will be settled in full and the fees for these payments will be deducted from the Fee Credit of INR 100.

blocks.clause_path_text: Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > CHARGEBACKS

blocks.text: If a Facility Provider communicates to Razorpay PA the receipt of a Chargeback Request, You will be notified of the Chargeback. You agree that liability for Chargeback, whether domestic or international, under the Terms solely rests with You. You further agree that it is Your sole discretion whether to avail non-3D secure services or not. Subject to availability of funds, Razorpay PA upon receipt of a Chargeback Request shall forthwith deduct Chargeback Amount from the Transaction Amounts, which may be used, based on the decision of the Facility Provider, either to a) process Chargeback in favour of the customer or b) credit to You. For the avoidance of doubt, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct the Chargeback Amount upon receiving a Chargeback claim. You shall be entitled to furnish to Razorpay PA documents and information ("Chargeback Documents") pertaining to the Transaction associated with the Chargeback Request in order to substantiate (i) the completion of the aforesaid Transaction; and /or; (ii) delivery of goods/services sought by the customer pursuant to the said Transaction. You shall furnish the Chargeback Documents within three (3) calendar days (or such other period specified by the Facility Provider) of receiving notification of the Chargeback Request.
blocks.text_without_marker: If a Facility Provider communicates to Razorpay PA the receipt of a Chargeback Request, You will be notified of the Chargeback. You agree that liability for Chargeback, whether domestic or international, under the Terms solely rests with You. You further agree that it is Your sole discretion whether to avail non-3D secure services or not. Subject to availability of funds, Razorpay PA upon receipt of a Chargeback Request shall forthwith deduct Chargeback Amount from the Transaction Amounts, which may be used, based on the decision of the Facility Provider, either to a) process Chargeback in favour of the customer or b) credit to You. For the avoidance of doubt, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct the Chargeback Amount upon receiving a Chargeback claim. You shall be entitled to furnish to Razorpay PA documents and information ("Chargeback Documents") pertaining to the Transaction associated with the Chargeback Request in order to substantiate (i) the completion of the aforesaid Transaction; and /or; (ii) delivery of goods/services sought by the customer pursuant to the said Transaction. You shall furnish the Chargeback Documents within three (3) calendar days (or such other period specified by the Facility Provider) of receiving notification of the Chargeback Request.

blocks.text: Notwithstanding anything in these Terms, if the Facility Providers charge the Chargeback Amount from Razorpay PA then You agree and acknowledge that Razorpay PA is entitled to recover such Chargeback Amount from You by way of deduction from (i) the Transaction Amounts to be settled to You and (ii) any of Your other funds held by Razorpay PA in the course of providing the Services. blocks.text: On the issuance of notice of termination under the Terms, Razorpay PA reserves the right to withhold from each settlement made during the notice period, a sum computed based on a Stipulated Percentage (defined hereinbelow) for a period of one hundred and twenty (120) days ("Withholding Term") from the date of termination of these Terms. The sums so withheld shall be utilized towards settlement of Chargebacks. After processing such Chargebacks, Razorpay PA shall transfer the unutilized amounts, if any, to You forthwith upon completion of the Withholding Term. The ‘Stipulated Percentage' is the proportion of the Chargeback Amounts out of the total Transaction Amounts settled during the subsistence of these Terms.

blocks.text: You represent and warrant that (i) You shall during the entire term of the usage of the Services, implement, observe and comply with applicable requirements prescribed under Applicable Laws, including but not limited to the provisions of the Payment Aggregator Guidelines. You shall further ensure that Your operations are in compliance with the Payment Aggregator Guidelines and You shall not undertake any action in breach of the same (ii) You shall on Your website/web app/mobile site/mobile app/ other modes of customer interface clearly indicate/display (a) the return and refund policy of Your products/services to Your customers, including the timelines for processing such returns, refunds or cancellations; and (b) the general terms of use and conditions of use by Your customers. You shall ensure that You deliver products and services in accordance with instructions of the customers. (iii) You shall at no time hold, store, copy or keep any customer data relating to a customer's Payment Instrument and shall notify in writing to Razorpay without any delay if You suspect or have become aware of a possible security breach related to any customer data. (iv) You shall not store any data pertaining to the Payment Instrument / customer Payment Instrument credentials or access any database or server containing any data pertaining to Payment Instrument/Payment Instrument credentials. On demand, You shall provide a written confirmation, in a form and manner acceptable to Razorpay PA and Facility Providers, certifying compliance to this aspect.

blocks.text: 1.1 The terms and conditions set forth in this Part IA apply exclusively to You when You utilize the Devices and/or use offline payment aggregation (PA-P) (defined as facilitating transactions where both the acceptance device and payment instrument are physically present in close proximity while making the transaction). This Part IA shall be read in conjunction with and is supplementary to the Part A (General Terms and Conditions) and Part I (Payment Aggregation Services) of these Terms. All terms and conditions detailed in Part A and Part I remain in full force and effect. In the event of any irreconcilable conflict between the express provisions of this Part IA and those of Part I or Part A, the provisions of this Part IA shall prevail only to the extent necessary to govern the specific requirements of offline payment aggregation. Your access to or use of the Device or offline payment aggregation services confirms Your full, binding acceptance of these additional terms.

This Part IA shall be read in conjunction with and is supplementary to the Part A (General Terms and Conditions) and Part I (Payment Aggregation Services) of these Terms. All terms and conditions detailed in Part A and Part I remain in full force and effect. In the event of any irreconcilable conflict between the express provisions of this Part IA and those of Part I or Part A, the provisions of this Part IA shall prevail only to the extent necessary to govern the specific requirements of offline payment aggregation. Your access to or use of the Device or offline payment aggregation services confirms Your full, binding acceptance of these additional terms.

blocks.text: 1.1 The terms and conditions set forth in this Part IB apply exclusively to You when You are a Merchant based outside of India (including overseas seller, marketplace and/or payment service providers) utilizing the Services to facilitate the collection of funds from customers within India (" PA-CB Outward Transactions "). This Part IB shall be read in conjunction with and is supplementary to the Part A (General Terms and Conditions) and Part I (Payment Aggregation Services) of these Terms. All terms and conditions detailed in Part A and Part I remain in full force and effect. However, You expressly acknowledge and agree that the specific operational, regulatory, and settlement requirements for cross-border payments necessitate specialized rules. Accordingly, where any express provision of this Part IB conflicts with or provides a different regulatory/operational requirement than the terms in Part I or Part A, the provisions of this Part IB shall prevail and supersede the conflicting provision to the extent necessary to govern the PA-CB Outward Transactions.

All references to "Razorpay PA", "us," or "we," or "our " under these Terms will automatically and exclusively mean and refer to the Transferor Entity till the Transfer Date and to the Transferee Entity from the Transfer Date. clauses.text: 2.3 By using the Services and providing your contact information, you consent to receiving information about, and offers for, various products and services from Razorpay PA, the Group Entities and/or third parties. You agree that Razorpay PA may contact you electronically or by phone to gauge your interest in specific products and services and to process your requests or applications. Additionally, you authorize Razorpay PA, along with its partners, service providers, vendors,, to contact you for purposes such as (i) presenting or soliciting your interest in other products or services from third parties, or (ii) sending marketing materials, offers, or other information through the Website or via other methods including telephone, SMS, email, WhatsApp, or other messaging services or digital means. You consent to receiving these communications on the phone or mobile number you provided on the website and explicitly waive any registration or preferences listed under the Do Not Disturb (DND) or National Customer Preference Register (NCPR) in accordance with Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) regulations.

In the event Your KYC Documents are not available in CKYCR, or if the retrieved record is not updated or is incomplete, You shall provide the necessary documents to enable Razorpay PA to conduct/complete the due diligence in respect of You and Your business / activities. Razorpay PA shall have the right to share the KYC Documents (or the information therein) and other related documents with the Facility Providers or governmental authorities or law enforcement agencies or the Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND) , as required under the Applicable Laws. You further acknowledge and agree that Razorpay PA reserves the right at all times to monitor, review, retain and/or disclose any information in relation to the Service(s) as necessary to satisfy any Applicable Laws, legal process or governmental request. You agree to promptly update Razorpay PA of any change in Your Information, promptly upon such change taking effect.

clauses.text: 3.1 Applicable fees for the provision of Services/Devices shall be levied by Razorpay PA from time to time. You agree that the fees shall be charged according to the manner, rates and frequency determined by Razorpay PA. Razorpay PA reserves the right to update the amount of the fees charged at its sole discretion. Razorpay PA fees allow access to the entire suite of payments products, dashboard and custom reports, and includes MDR charges, if any, for payment instruments as prescribed under Applicable Law. For clarity, Razorpay PA fees include zero MDR for Rupay Debit Cards and UPI transactions.

For disputed invoices, Razorpay PA shall use good faith efforts to reconcile any reasonably disputed amounts.

clauses.text: 3.5 For fees deducted upfront before provision of the specific Service, it is agreed that if You deposit applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in respect of invoices received by You) and furnish to Razorpay PA Form 16-A in respect of such taxes paid, then Razorpay PA shall reimburse to You, on a quarterly basis, the amount in respect of such taxes paid. In all other cases, with respect to invoices received by You, at the time of payment of the Fees, You will withhold applicable taxes under Section 194H of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (in case LTDC is provided as per the LTDC issued). You shall deposit the withheld taxes with the government treasury, file the statutorily mandated returns and furnish the requisite tax deduction certificate (Form 16-A) to Razorpay PA within one hundred and eighty (180) days so as to enable Razorpay PA to obtain full credit for the taxes deducted at source.

clauses.text: 3.6 If You operate as an e-commerce operator, facilitating the sale of goods or provisions of services of a resident e-commerce participant, You shall evaluate and comply with the requirements of TDS under Section 19.40 of the Income Tax Act, 1961. You, as an e-commerce operator, will withhold tax under Section 19.40 of the Act and deposit the same within the applicable timelines, including carrying out all the necessary compliances as prescribed under the Income Tax Act, 1961. As the payment service provider, Razorpay PA would not be obligated to deduct tax under Section 19.40 and the same will be Your responsibility as the e-commerce operator. For the purposes of this clause 3.5A, "e-commerce operator" and "e-commerce participant" shall have the meaning assigned to them in section 19.40 of the Income Tax Act, 1961.

clauses.text: 3.7 You shall be solely responsible for updating Your GST registration number on the Razorpay PA dashboard before Razorpay PA generates the invoice and shall also submit the GST certificate as part of KYC. Razorpay PA will raise a GST tax invoice and report the transactions in the GST returns based on the information provided by You. The GST returns will be filed as per the statutory timelines, to enable You to avail appropriate input tax credit. Razorpay PA shall not be responsible for any mistake and or misrepresentation by You in updating the GST number and other particulars as per the GST certificate. Further, any liability raised on Razorpay PA by the GST authorities due to incorrect information provided by You or deliberate withholding of any statutory information by You shall be recovered by Razorpay PA from You.

clauses.text: 13.1 You agree to comply with all applicable anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws which prohibit officials, representatives, agents or any other person associated with or acting on behalf of You from giving, offering, promising to offer, receiving/ accepting or acting in any other manner so as to induce a payment, gift, hospitality or anything else of value (either directly or indirectly) whether from within the country or from abroad to government officials, public servants, regulatory bodies, judicial authorities, persons in positions of authority, elected or contesting electoral candidates, political parties or office bearers thereof or any other third party or person in order to obtain an improper commercial/ business advantage of any kind. clauses.text: e. You may choose to purchase Fee Credits from Razorpay PA in respect of the online payment aggregation Services. It is agreed that in respect of each Transaction, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct an amount equivalent to Razorpay PA fees along with applicable taxes from the Fee Credit. You agree that if sufficient funds are not available in the Fee Credits, then Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct fees along with applicable taxes from the Transaction Amount. "Fee Credits" are the credits using which You may receive the full settlement amount without any fee deduction. For example, if You have a Fee Credit of INR 100 then all the Transactions will be settled in full and the fees for these payments will be deducted from the Fee Credit of INR 100.

clauses.text: If a Facility Provider communicates to Razorpay PA the receipt of a Chargeback Request, You will be notified of the Chargeback. You agree that liability for Chargeback, whether domestic or international, under the Terms solely rests with You. You further agree that it is Your sole discretion whether to avail non-3D secure services or not. Subject to availability of funds, Razorpay PA upon receipt of a Chargeback Request shall forthwith deduct Chargeback Amount from the Transaction Amounts, which may be used, based on the decision of the Facility Provider, either to a) process Chargeback in favour of the customer or b) credit to You. For the avoidance of doubt, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to deduct the Chargeback Amount upon receiving a Chargeback claim. You shall be entitled to furnish to Razorpay PA documents and information ("Chargeback Documents") pertaining to the Transaction associated with the Chargeback Request in order to substantiate (i) the completion of the aforesaid Transaction; and /or; (ii) delivery of goods/services sought by the customer pursuant to the said Transaction. You shall furnish the Chargeback Documents within three (3) calendar days (or such other period specified by the Facility Provider) of receiving notification of the Chargeback Request.

clauses.text: On the issuance of notice of termination under the Terms, Razorpay PA reserves the right to withhold from each settlement made during the notice period, a sum computed based on a Stipulated Percentage (defined hereinbelow) for a period of one hundred and twenty (120) days ("Withholding Term") from the date of termination of these Terms. The sums so withheld shall be utilized towards settlement of Chargebacks. After processing such Chargebacks, Razorpay PA shall transfer the unutilized amounts, if any, to You forthwith upon completion of the Withholding Term. The ‘Stipulated Percentage' is the proportion of the Chargeback Amounts out of the total Transaction Amounts settled during the subsistence of these Terms.
